Projects

01

Urban Traffic Simulation with NVIDIA Omniverse

NVIDIA Omniverse USD Python Simulation Digital Twin
+

Urban traffic simulation is traditionally built using large game engines or specialised simulation frameworks — powerful systems, but often tightly coupled, centrally controlled, and expensive to maintain. This project explores a different architecture entirely.

"Can believable city traffic be simulated using a modular scene-driven architecture instead of a traditional engine-based simulation loop?"

The answer is a modular urban driving simulation built with NVIDIA Omniverse and Universal Scene Description (USD) — a lightweight environment where vehicle behaviour, traffic control, and intersection logic operate as independent components communicating through the scene itself.

Core Concept: Scene-Driven Simulation

The key design goal was to avoid a centralised simulation controller altogether. Instead, the simulation uses USD as a live data layer — a shared space where components publish and read state.

  • Scene objects publish their state through USD attributes
  • Behaviour scripts read those attributes and respond accordingly
  • No central simulation controller is required

In practice: traffic lights publish their signal state; vehicles read it to decide whether to stop; intersections maintain queue data that vehicles query. The scene becomes the configuration. The code becomes the behaviour.

What the Simulation Does

  • Vehicles follow spline-based road networks
  • Traffic lights dynamically control vehicle flow
  • All-way stop intersections manage vehicle queues and right-of-way
  • Vehicles implement car-following behaviour and adjust speed automatically
  • Randomised speed parameters create natural traffic variation
  • Multiple camera modes allow observation of the system from different perspectives

System Architecture

ComponentRole
VehicleControllerSpawns vehicles and manages the active pool within the simulation
VehiclePath following, speed control, car-following logic, reaction to signals and intersections
TrafficLightPublishes signal state to USD scene for vehicles to read
AllStopIntersectionMaintains a vehicle queue and manages right-of-way assignment

Each component is implemented as a BehaviorScript attached to a scene prim in Omniverse. Because each script operates independently and reads data from the scene, the system remains highly modular and extensible.

Tech Stack

ComponentTechnology
Simulation PlatformNVIDIA Omniverse
Scene DescriptionUSD (Universal Scene Description)
ScriptingPython (BehaviorScript)
Road NetworksSpline-based path system
3D AssetsSketchfab community models

Advantages of USD-Based Architecture

🧩

Modular System Design

Behaviour is encapsulated in independent scripts attached to scene objects — no central coordinator needed.

🗂️

Scene-Driven Configuration

Simulation parameters can be adjusted directly within the USD scene without code changes.

🔌

Easy Extensibility

New behaviours can be added as new BehaviorScripts without modifying existing components.

📡

Synthetic Data Compatibility

USD environments integrate naturally with autonomous vehicle perception data generation pipelines.

Future Development

  • Autonomous ego vehicle with route planning
  • Distinct NPC driving behaviours — lane changes, overtaking
  • Pedestrian agents interacting with crosswalks
  • Synthetic data generation for AV perception model training
  • Automatic OpenStreetMap to USD road network generation

02

Virtual Model Factory

Unity 3D C# Industrial Simulation VR-Ready Digital Twin
+

A real-time 3D simulation of an automotive manufacturing plant, built in Unity 3D. The application reproduces the full production environment — assembly lines, robotic welding cells, press shops, material handling logistics and human operators — all within a navigable, first-person walk-through experience.

"Instead of stalking enemies you are tracking down and eliminating bottlenecks, waste and variability that impede the smooth running of a factory."

Developed to support industrial digitalisation: giving engineers, planners and training teams the ability to explore, analyse and improve factory operations without stepping onto a physical shop floor.

What the Simulation Covers

  • Body Shop — robotic welding stations assembling car body panels and structural components
  • Press Shop — automated stamping lines producing door panels and chassis parts
  • Assembly Line — multi-station line with animated operators fitting doors and panels to moving vehicle bodies
  • Logistics & Material Handling — forklifts transporting component racks between stations
  • Safety & Compliance Layer — floor markings, station signage, fire safety posters throughout

Visual Walkthrough

Assembly Line — Station View

Station 2 — Main Assembly LineOperators working on car door panels suspended from an overhead power-and-free conveyor. Safety signage and SOP boards on the rear wall.
Assembly Hall — Wide AngleMultiple operators active simultaneously. Overhead conveyor track, gantry structures and safety fencing at accurate scale.
Logistics ZoneForklift transporting component racks. Worker at machine terminal. Outdoor environment visible through bay doors.
Robot Welding CellTwo industrial robot arms on safety platform, performing welding operations on a car body component.
Press ShopWorker inspecting stacked stamped components. Racks of door panels and body frames in foreground.
Press Line HMI StationOperator at control terminal alongside multi-stage automated press line.
Welding Fixture StationOne worker positions a car door frame in a holding jig while a second performs spot welding. Andon stack and fire safety boards visible.

Tech Stack

ComponentTechnologyPurpose
Game EngineUnity 3DReal-time 3D rendering, simulation, navigation
LanguageC#Simulation logic, NPC behaviour, interaction systems
AnimationUnity AnimatorWorker movements, robot arm cycles, conveyor motion
PhysicsUnity PhysXCollision, movement, ergonomic simulation
PlatformWindows / VR-ReadyWalkthroughs, training sessions, planning reviews

Key Features

🚶

Full Plant Navigation

Walk freely through every zone in first-person, exactly as on a real shop floor visit.

⚙️

Animated Production Flow

Workers, robots, conveyors and forklifts all move continuously, simulating live production cycles.

🗺️

Multi-Zone Coverage

Assembly line, body shop, press shop, welding cells and logistics in one continuous environment.

🛡️

Ergonomic & Safety Review

Human character animations and station layouts support ergonomic assessment and safety planning.

📋

Station-Level Detail

Every station includes signage, SOP boards, safety markings and equipment labels matching real plant standards.

📡

Training & Onboarding

Remote stakeholders learn plant layout and safety protocols without a physical site visit.

03

Virtual Home Configurator

Unity 3D C# 3D Visualisation Product Configuration
+

An interactive 3D room planning and furniture pre-visualisation tool that lets customers design their home layout, furnish it from a real product catalog, and walk through the configured space in first-person — all before making a purchase decision.

"Customers can design an entire room from scratch, place real furniture, see how it looks at human scale, and proceed directly to order — eliminating guesswork and reducing purchase hesitation."

Application Walkthrough

Stage 1 — Room Layout & Wall Construction

Users start on a 2D top-down grid, drawing their floor plan by placing walls with precise measurements. The Walls panel offers thickness choices (1", 6", 12") and exact lengths in feet, with a real-time dimension overlay.

2D Floor PlanThree rooms defined on a 28×28ft grid. Straight Wall panel with thickness options and floor/roof generator.
Flooring & Window SelectionFloor textures applied per room. Others Library panel shows a window product ready to place.

Stage 2 — Furniture from Product Catalog

Users switch to the Product Library — furniture organised by category and concept line — and place items into the floor plan. The Configuration panel logs each product with a unique ID, ready for order processing.

Top-Down Furniture PlacementBedroom furniture placed and visible from above. Configuration panel on the right logs each product with a unique ID.

Stage 3 — First-Person Walk-Through

Walk Mode delivers an immersive first-person view of the configured space. Camera height is adjustable (3ft / 5ft / 7ft) and a live minimap tracks position at all times.

Living RoomSofa, wall artwork, AC and TV rendered at true scale. Teal accent wall and warm timber floor.
BedroomWardrobe, dressing mirror, bedside unit and bed at human scale. Teal and olive colour scheme.
Hallway NavigationRoom-to-room transitions through a rendered door. Minimap shows current position and orientation.

Key Features

📐

Precision Floor Plan Builder

Draw walls with exact measurements in feet. Choose thickness. Generate floors and roofs with one click.

🚪

Doors, Windows & Openings

Place openings from a product library onto any wall segment in the 2D plan view.

🛋️

Real Product Catalog

Furniture organised by category and concept line, with unique product IDs for order tracking.

🗂️

Live Configuration Panel

Every item placed is logged with product name and unique ID — ready to push to an order.

👁️

First-Person Walk-Through

Switch from 2D plan to immersive walk mode. Adjust camera height (3ft / 5ft / 7ft).

🗺️

Real-Time Minimap

Live floor plan minimap showing position and orientation during walk mode.

Impact & Value

  • Reduces purchase hesitation by letting customers validate layout and furniture scale
  • Enables sales teams to guide customers through design sessions in-store
  • Connects directly to order management via the live configuration panel
  • Positions the brand as a digitally forward furniture retailer
04

Design Your Jewelry Personal

Unity 3D C# 3D Visualisation Product Customisation
+

An interactive desktop application that lets customers design their own custom jewelry piece from scratch — choosing pendant shape, gold colour (Yellow / White / Rose), bail style and gemstones — before naming and ordering their one-of-a-kind creation.

"Bring your creation to life — design something that is entirely, unmistakably yours."

How It Works

The application walks customers through five sequential stages, each updating a central high-fidelity 3D preview in real time:

  1. Customer Registration — profile linked to the final design for order follow-up
  2. Shape & Gold Colour — browse pendant forms; choose Yellow, White or Rose gold; live price updates
  3. Bail Selection — pick the connector style that attaches the pendant to a chain
  4. Gemstone Selection — Diamonds or Coloured Stones, multiple cut shapes and sizes, placed live on the pendant
  5. Name Your Creation — personalise the piece before saving and ordering
Shape & Gold SelectionPendant rendered live in 3D as the customer browses shapes and gold colours.
Stone Placed — Order ReadyBlue Topaz set on the completed pendant. Price and full specification captured for the jeweler.

Key Features

  • Real-Time 3D Preview — every choice updates the photorealistic render instantly
  • Three Gold Colours — Yellow, White and Rose gold rendered accurately
  • Dual Stone Category — Diamonds and Coloured Stones with multiple cut shapes and size variants
  • Live Pricing — total cost updates with every configuration change
  • Named Creation & Order Output — fully specified design record handed off directly to the jeweler
